title: RSA integration test is a self-oracle (no independent verification)
description: tests/integration.rs rsa_round_trip encrypts with wolfhsm PublicEncrypt and decrypts with wolfhsm PrivateDecrypt using the same key. This is a self-oracle: it tests only that the HSM can reverse its own operation, not that the operation is correct per any standard. A bug that corrupts both encrypt and decrypt symmetrically would pass. Consequence: sets a pattern that contributors will copy for future key types; weakens regression signal. Fix: cross-validate against a pure-Rust RSA crate (e.g. rsa crate) or known PKCS#1 test vectors.
